meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve slow development speed (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Speed Up Your Development (Student Guide)
content.seo_description: Overcome slow development speed with this intermediate guide. Master AI-assisted debugging using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your SaaS projects faster.
content.answer_block: Slow development speed stems from manual debugging loops that kill your momentum. By integrating AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code into your CLI workflow, you shift from line-by-line troubleshooting to architectural reasoning, allowing you to resolve complex bugs in seconds while leveling up your system design skills.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal that won’t stop spitting out stack traces, and the clock is ticking on your project deadline. That familiar friction—the 'slow development crawl'—is exactly what separates hobbyists from professional software engineers. You feel like you're spending more time fighting the compiler than actually building features that matter.

But the era of manual, exhaustive debugging is over. By treating AI as a pair-programmer that lives inside your terminal, you transform your workflow from a struggle into a high-velocity sprint. This guide isn't about code snippets; it's about mastering the tools that will redefine how you build, debug, and ship your first real-world applications.

steps.title: Contextualize the Environment
steps.description: Before debugging, ensure the AI understands your stack. Feed it your project structure to prevent hallucinated solutions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project directory structure. Identify potential bottlenecks in my current Node.js setup and suggest a standard debugging workflow for this architecture.

steps.title: Automated Trace Analysis
steps.description: Inject your error logs directly into the AI to pinpoint the exact line of failure rather than guessing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I have a recurring memory leak in my service. Analyze these logs [paste logs] and identify the specific function causing the heap overflow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the last 50 lines of error.log and propose a fix for the identified crash.'

steps.title: Refactoring for Debuggability
steps.description: Use AI to instrument your code with better logging and error boundaries, preventing future slow-downs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module to include robust error handling and structured logging that makes future debugging easier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Add Winston logging to the auth middleware and implement error boundaries.'

steps.title: Automated Test Generation
steps.description: Don't just fix the bug—write a test that ensures it never returns. Use AI to bridge the gap between fix and validation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest unit test for the function we just debugged to verify the edge case that caused the crash.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a test file for the recently modified auth service.'

steps.title: Architectural Review
steps.description: Perform a final sweep to ensure your fix doesn't introduce technical debt or performance regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the recent changes for performance regressions. Does this fix impact the latency of the main request pipeline?

pro_tips: Always use the '--diff' flag when applying AI fixes to manually verify changes before merging.
Keep your context window clean by regularly clearing logs and old terminal history from your agent's memory.
Treat AI suggestions as a hypothesis, not a source of truth—always run the tests before deploying.
Use 'Chain of Thought' prompting: ask the AI to explain its reasoning before it generates the code.
Leverage local documentation indexing so the AI understands your specific library versions and custom API wrappers.
resources: Effective Prompt Engineering for Devs: https://learn.prompting.guide
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, integrate your AI-assisted workflow into a CI/CD pipeline to automate the testing phase, ensuring your builds are always production-ready.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop fighting your code and start scaling your output. The gap between student and lead engineer is simply the quality of your workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
